spec_match.rationale: The page now loads a server-streamed HTML document whose bytes contain processing-instruction ranges and literal <template for> patches for hero, related, and repeated results.

interactive.rationale: Controls reload the live route and inspect streamed bytes, but native Chrome patch application still needs chrome-devtools-mcp verification.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The copy now describes declarative partial updates instead of generic ReadableStream dispatch behavior.

edge_cases.rationale: The live route covers out-of-DOM-order patches, repeated marker appends, configurable slow chunks, and unsupported-browser fallback detection.

references.rationale: The page links to ChromeStatus, the WICG patching explainer, and the WHATWG HTML PR.

design_system.rationale: The rebuilt page uses shared CSS variables, responsive controls, and explicit foreground/background colors for local button variants.

openQuestions.title: Verify native parser patching in Chrome 150
openQuestions.detail: Open the page with chrome-devtools-mcp, reload the live stream, and confirm the iframe applies <template for> patches without leaving fallback placeholders or unapplied template elements.

summary: Rebuilt the concept around the actual server-streamed <template for> parser patch mechanism. Static and route-level verification is complete; browser verification is pending because chrome-devtools-mcp is unavailable.
